what_it_does: This file implements a TypeScript module that spawns the local codex app-server, speaks JSON-RPC over stdio, requests a hooks/list for a given cwd, filters hooks that belong to episodic-memory, reads their trust/trustStatus/trust_status fields, and returns a normalized CodexHookTrustState.
observations: Uses JSON-RPC over child process stdio to talk to codex app-server.
Implements a small request/response dispatcher with a Map of pending calls keyed by numeric id.
Filters hooks by checking whether pluginId or key starts with episodic-memory@.
Accepts three possible trust-status field names: trustStatus, trust, and trust_status.
Aggregates trust states across all matching hooks with precedence trusted/managed > modified > untrusted > unknown.
Returns not_found when no episodic-memory hooks are present.
Falls back to unknown on parse failures, RPC errors, or timeout.
Sends initialize/initialized handshake before calling hooks/list.
standouts: The trust-state aggregation uses a precedence rule rather than requiring unanimity among matching hooks.
The hook ownership test is a simple prefix heuristic on pluginId or key.
It wraps an external CLI as a short-lived JSON-RPC client.
conventions: Follows TypeScript strict unknown handling with a Record<string, unknown> type guard.
Uses async/await with try/finally for process and timeout cleanup.
Prefers defensive parsing and returning a sentinel unknown state over throwing.
concerns: A single trusted/managed hook can mask other untrusted or modified hooks in the same list.
Trust status is inferred from whichever of three differently-named fields happens to be present.
The codex binary is invoked by bare name, relying on PATH.
The timeout kills the child but pending promises are rejected with a generic message; there is no explicit stdin/end-of-stream handling.
notes: A small adapter that turns Codex hook metadata into a single episodic-memory trust-state answer.
questions: What does the codex app-server hooks/list response schema look like in practice?
Should managed be treated as fully equivalent to trusted, or as a separate state?
How should conflicting trust states across multiple hooks be resolved?
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to give the recollection system a reliable, file-local way to ask Codex whether its episodic-memory hooks are trusted, without hard-coding a single trust field name or response shape.
contribution: A self-contained module that bridges Codex CLI JSON-RPC to a typed episodic-memory hook trust-state answer, including request dispatch, response parsing, and a normalized status mapping.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that queries the codex app-server over stdio JSON-RPC for hooks/list, filters episodic-memory hooks, and derives a normalized trust state from their trust-related fields.
intention_for_embedding: To let the recollection package determine whether its Codex episodic-memory hooks are trusted, untrusted, modified, missing, or unknown at runtime.

symbols.does: Defines the union of possible trust-state return values.
symbols.description: A string-literal union type representing the outcomes trusted, untrusted, modified, not_found, and unknown that the module can report after inspecting Codex hooks.

symbols.does: Describes the shape of JSON-RPC messages read from the codex app-server stdout.
symbols.description: An interface with optional id, result, and error fields used to parse and dispatch lines received from the spawned codex process.

symbols.does: Type guard that checks whether a value is a non-null object.
symbols.description: A narrow helper that asserts a value is a Record<string, unknown>, enabling safe property access during untrusted JSON parsing.

symbols.does: Returns true when a hook object appears to belong to the episodic-memory plugin.
symbols.description: Inspects the pluginId and key fields of a hook and returns true if either starts with episodic-memory@, serving as the ownership filter for trust-state extraction.

symbols.does: Parses a hooks/list response and returns the normalized trust state for episodic-memory hooks.
symbols.description: Walks nested entries and hooks, collects hooks that belong to episodic-memory, reads trustStatus/trust/trust_status strings, and applies a precedence-based aggregation to produce a single CodexHookTrustState.

symbols.does: Spawns codex app-server and asks it for the trust state of hooks in a given cwd.
symbols.description: An async function that launches the codex CLI with JSON-RPC over stdio, performs initialize/initialized handshake, calls hooks/list, and delegates trust-state derivation to trustStateFromHooksList, falling back to unknown on failure or timeout.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON-RPC notification to the codex child process.
symbols.description: A local closure that writes a JSON object with a method but no id to child.stdin, used to send the initialized notification after the initialize request/response.
